title: ExpenseX – Full-Stack Personal Finance App

requirements.text: Implement Internet Identity-based secure login. Unauthenticated users see a login screen; authenticated users see the main app. Store each user's data scoped to their principal.
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users cannot access any app data or screens.
Login via Internet Identity works and redirects to the Home dashboard.
Each user's accounts, transactions, categories, and budgets are isolated by principal.

requirements.text: Implement multi-account management. Support account types: Cash, Bank Account, UPI, Credit Card, and Custom. Each account stores a name, type, and running balance. Users can add a new account, delete an account, view its balance, and transfer money between two accounts (which creates a Transfer transaction).
acceptanceCriteria: User can create an account specifying name, type, and initial balance.
User can delete an account (with confirmation).
Account list shows each account with its current computed balance.
Transfer between accounts deducts from source and adds to destination, and appears as a Transfer record in both accounts.
All five account types (Cash, Bank Account, UPI, Credit Card, Custom) are supported.

requirements.text: Implement a Transactions/Records system. Each transaction has a type (Income, Expense, or Transfer), an account, a category, an amount, and a date/time. Support: add transaction, delete transaction, search transactions by keyword, filter by date range / category / account, and sort by amount or date ascending/descending.
acceptanceCriteria: User can add an Income, Expense, or Transfer transaction with account, category, amount, and date/time.
User can delete any transaction.
Search input filters the displayed transaction list by keyword.
Filter controls allow filtering by date range, category, and account simultaneously.
Sort controls allow sorting by amount (asc/desc) and date (asc/desc).
Account balances update correctly when transactions are added or deleted.

requirements.text: Implement Category Management. Pre-populate default categories: Food, Travel, Shopping, Rent, Salary, Bills, EMI, Entertainment, Health, Other. Users can add custom categories, edit existing category names, and delete categories.
acceptanceCriteria: All 10 default categories exist on first launch for a new user.
User can add a new custom category with a name.
User can edit an existing category's name.
User can delete a category (with confirmation).
Categories are available for selection when adding a transaction.

requirements.text: Implement Budget Management. Users can set an overall monthly budget and per-category monthly budgets. Display remaining budget and percentage used for the current month. Show a warning when 80% of a budget is consumed, and a red alert when a budget is exceeded.
acceptanceCriteria: User can set a total monthly budget amount.
User can set a budget for each individual category.
Budget screen shows remaining amount and a progress bar with percentage used.
An amber/yellow warning indicator appears when usage reaches 80%.
A red alert indicator appears when spending exceeds the budget.
Budgets are evaluated against expense transactions for the current calendar month.

requirements.text: Implement an Analysis & Reports screen with a dashboard summary (Total Balance, Total Income, Total Expense), a monthly overview bar chart (income vs expense per month), a pie chart for category-wise expenses, a line graph for spending trend over time, and a weekly/monthly/yearly filter. Include a button to download a simple PDF report of transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows correct totals for balance, income, and expense.
Bar chart displays monthly income vs expense for the selected period.
Pie chart shows expense distribution across categories.
Line graph shows spending trend over the selected time range.
Weekly, monthly, and yearly filter buttons change all charts accordingly.
A 'Download PDF' button generates and downloads a PDF summary of the filtered transactions.

requirements.text: Implement a Settings screen with: currency selection (default ₹ INR) from a list of common currencies, and language toggle between English and Hindi. The selected currency symbol must appear throughout the app wherever amounts are displayed.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings screen has a currency selector defaulting to ₹ (INR).
Changing currency updates the symbol displayed next to all amounts app-wide.
Settings screen has a language toggle between English and Hindi.
Selected language and currency preferences persist across sessions.

requirements.text: Implement a bottom navigation bar with five tabs: Home, Records, Add (a prominent center '+' FAB button that opens the add-transaction modal), Analysis, and Accounts. Navigation must be responsive and work on both mobile and desktop widths.
acceptanceCriteria: A bottom navigation bar is always visible.
Five tabs are present: Home, Records, a center '+' button, Analysis, Accounts.
Tapping the '+' button opens the Add Transaction modal/sheet.
Active tab is visually highlighted.
Navigation works correctly on both narrow (mobile) and wide (desktop) viewports.

requirements.text: Apply a clean finance-themed visual design using a Green and Teal/Blue color palette with dark mode support, smooth CSS/Framer-style transitions on page and modal changes, and a fully responsive layout optimized for mobile-first use.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary colors are green and teal/blue throughout the UI.
A dark mode toggle exists (in Settings or a header button) and persists preference.
Page and modal transitions use smooth animations (fade/slide).
Layout is fully usable on mobile screen widths (375px+) and adapts gracefully on desktop.

constraints: All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor (backend/main.mo).
Do not edit any file under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui.
No external databases or third-party APIs; all data must be stored on-chain in the Motoko actor.
PDF generation must be client-side only (no backend involvement).
Real-time WebSocket/push notifications are not supported; use polling or in-app alerts only.
nonGoals: Multi-user shared accounts or family mode.
Bank API or UPI API integration for automatic transaction import.
Edit transaction (only add and delete for this build).
Edit account (only add and delete for this build).

required: ExpenseX app logo — a modern minimal icon with a stylized 'X' inside a rounded square, green-to-teal gradient background, white foreground mark (expensex-logo.dim_256x256.png)
